questions.prompt: When should you migrate from useState to useReducer?

choices.text: When state updates become tangled: 5+ useState calls with interdependencies; state fields that always update together (loading + data + error); complex transitions (form steps, wizard flows); state that depends on previous state in complex ways; need to test transitions separately. useReducer consolidates transitions in one pure function — easier to reason about, easier to test, and dispatch has a stable reference (doesn't trigger child re-renders the way new callbacks do). Start with useState; migrate when complexity warrants.

questions.explanation: useReducer when state transitions are coordinated, complex, or many — consolidates logic.

questions.prompt: Form initialized from prop `initialValue`. Prop changes later but the form still shows the original value. Why?

choices.text: `useState(initialValue)` only uses the argument on the first render — it's the initial state, not a subscription. Subsequent prop changes don't update state because state is local now. Two fixes: (1) Use `key={someStableId}` on the form component — changing the key remounts the form with the new initial value, cleanly resetting all internal state. (2) Treat the form as controlled — derive value from props directly, fire onChange to parent. Pick based on whether you want the form to own the state (key reset) or the parent (controlled).

questions.explanation: useState(initial) only fires on first render. Use key={id} to remount, or make the form controlled.
